column2: Rename the file at ~/Desktop/kinbench/001-input.txt to 001-output.txt (keep it in the same folder).
Create a new note in the Notes app titled 'KinBench Test 003' (any body content is fine).
Switch macOS to Dark Mode (use System Settings → Appearance).
There are three files in ~/Desktop/kinbench/005-archive/ (a.txt, b.txt, c.txt). Compress them into a single zip archive named ~/Desktop/kinbench/005-archive.zip.
Create a new folder named '006-myfolder' in ~/Desktop/kinbench/.
Move the file ~/Desktop/kinbench/007-doomed.txt to the Trash.
Move the file file.txt from ~/Desktop/kinbench/008-src/ to ~/Desktop/kinbench/008-dst/.
Duplicate the file at ~/Desktop/kinbench/009-source.txt — leave both the original and the duplicate in the same folder.

There are three files in ~/Desktop/kinbench/010-batch/ that all start with 'prefix-' (prefix-A.txt, prefix-B.txt, prefix-C.txt). Rename all of them so they start with 'renamed-' instead (so prefix-A.txt → renamed-A.txt, etc).
Create a new note in Notes titled 'KinBench Test 014' with body content that includes the phrase 'agent benchmark validation'.
There's a note titled 'KinBench Test 015' in Notes that currently contains the line 'original line'. Open it and append a new line that says 'appended line' (so the final note has both lines).
In Mail, create a new email draft with the subject 'KinBench 016 Test' and the body 'This is a test draft from kin-bench.' Don't send it — leave it as a draft.
In Calendar, find the event titled 'KinBench Find Me' that's scheduled for tomorrow. Note the time it starts and write that time (in HH:MM 24-hour format) to ~/Desktop/kinbench/017-found-time.txt.
Create a calendar event titled 'KinBench Lunch' for tomorrow at 12:30 PM (default duration is fine).
Create a calendar event titled 'KinBench Coffee' for the day after tomorrow at 9:00 AM, and set the location to 'Blue Bottle'.

There's an event titled 'KinBench Weekly Sync' next week in the Calendar. Find which day of the week it falls on and write the day name (Monday/Tuesday/Wednesday/Thursday/Friday/Saturday/Sunday) to ~/Desktop/kinbench/020-day.txt.
Turn on Do Not Disturb (or any Focus mode) using the Control Center icon in the menu bar (or System Settings > Focus).
Open System Settings and change the mouse tracking speed (also called pointer speed / tracking speed) to a new value — anything different from current is fine.
In System Settings > Lock Screen (or Desktop & Screen Saver), change the 'Start Screen Saver after' value to 5 minutes (or any non-current value if 5 isn't available).
Open the Terminal app and run the command 'pwd > ~/Desktop/kinbench/024-pwd.txt' so the current directory gets written to that file.
Open Terminal and create a file at ~/Desktop/kinbench/025-greeting.txt containing the single line 'hello from kinbench' (use any shell command — echo, cat heredoc, printf, whatever).
Use Terminal to count how many .txt files are in ~/Desktop/kinbench/026-input/ and write that number (just the digits, on a single line) to ~/Desktop/kinbench/026-count.txt.
Open Pages, create a new blank document, type 'Hello world from kinbench' as the body, and save the document to ~/Desktop/kinbench/027-doc.pages.
Open Numbers, create a new blank spreadsheet, type the value 42 into cell A1, and save the file to ~/Desktop/kinbench/028-sheet.numbers.

Take a screenshot of your screen, save it to ~/Desktop/kinbench/029-shot.png, then open Mail and create a new draft email with subject 'KinBench 029 Screenshot' that has that screenshot attached. Don't send the email — leave it as a draft.

There's a calendar event titled 'KinBench Source Event' tomorrow. Find that event, then open the Reminders app and create a new reminder with the same title ('KinBench Source Event'). The reminder doesn't need a due date.
In Finder, find the file ~/Desktop/kinbench/031-tag-me.txt and apply the Red color tag to it (right-click on the file → Tags → click the red dot).
In Finder, make an alias of ~/Desktop/kinbench/032-target.txt and place the alias in ~/Desktop/kinbench/032-aliases/ (use Finder File menu → Make Alias, or Cmd+L, or right-click).
Compress the entire folder ~/Desktop/kinbench/033-folder/ (with all its contents and nested subfolders) into a single archive at ~/Desktop/kinbench/033-folder.zip.
Delete the note titled 'KinBench Test 036 (delete me)' in the Notes app.
Find every note in the Notes app whose title starts with 'KinBench Listing' and write each title (one per line, just the titles) to ~/Desktop/kinbench/037-titles.txt.
There's an event titled 'KinBench Reschedule' tomorrow at 10:00 AM. Change its start time to 11:00 AM (same date, preserve duration).
Delete the event titled 'KinBench Cancel' from Calendar (it's scheduled for tomorrow afternoon).
Set the system output volume to 50% (use any method — menu bar volume slider, System Settings > Sound, or function keys).
Open System Settings > Desktop & Dock and set the bottom-right Hot Corner to 'Mission Control'.
Toggle the 'Natural scrolling' setting in System Settings > Trackpad (or Mouse) > Scroll & Zoom — it doesn't matter whether you turn it on or off, just flip whatever the current state is.
Open Terminal and create a new directory at ~/Desktop/kinbench/043-repo, cd into it, and initialize an empty git repository (git init).
Use Terminal to count how many lines in ~/Desktop/kinbench/044-input.txt contain the word 'kinbench' (case-sensitive) and write just that number to ~/Desktop/kinbench/044-count.txt.
In the Reminders app, create a new reminder titled 'KinBench Buy Milk' (no due date or priority needed).
In the Reminders app, mark the reminder titled 'KinBench Mark Done' as completed (click its checkbox).
There's a reminder titled 'KinBench Schedule' in the Reminders app. Set its due date to tomorrow at 5:00 PM (17:00).
Find the file ~/Desktop/kinbench/048-attach.txt in Finder, then open Mail and create a new draft email with subject 'KinBench 048' that has that file attached. Don't send it — leave it as a draft.
Use Spotlight (Cmd+Space) to launch the Calendar app, then create a new event titled 'KinBench Spotlight Path' for tomorrow at 3:00 PM.
Open Pages, create a new blank document containing the text 'kinbench 050 export', then export the document as a PDF to ~/Desktop/kinbench/050-doc.pdf (File menu → Export To → PDF).
Toggle the Finder sidebar visibility (View menu > Hide Sidebar / Show Sidebar).
Toggle the Finder status bar (View menu > Show / Hide Status Bar).
Toggle the Finder path bar (View menu > Show / Hide Path Bar).

Add the folder ~/Desktop/kinbench to the Finder sidebar Favorites (drag from Desktop into Favorites, or right-click → 'Add to Sidebar'). After adding, write the absolute folder path '/Users/<you>/Desktop/kinbench' to ~/Desktop/kinbench/055-favorites-confirm.txt — this is a confirmation file so the eval can verify you completed the action (the sidebar plist itself is TCC-protected).

Find every file in ~/Desktop/kinbench/ whose name contains 'kinbench-search-062' and write each matching path (one per line) to ~/Desktop/kinbench/062-search-results.txt. You can use Finder's Cmd+F search field or shell tools — whichever is faster. The eval verifies the result file's contents.

There is a file named 'kinbench-spotlight-063.txt' somewhere under ~/Desktop/kinbench/. Find it (use Spotlight Cmd+Space, or `mdfind`, or `find` — whatever is fastest), then write its absolute path to ~/Desktop/kinbench/063-found-path.txt.
Apply both the Yellow and Green color tags to the file ~/Desktop/kinbench/064-multi-tag.txt.
Remove all color tags from the file ~/Desktop/kinbench/065-tagged.txt.
Apply the Blue color tag (specifically Blue, not Red) to ~/Desktop/kinbench/066-target.txt.
Compress the 5 files in ~/Desktop/kinbench/067-files/ into a single archive at ~/Desktop/kinbench/067-archive.zip.
Extract the archive ~/Desktop/kinbench/068-bundle.zip into ~/Desktop/kinbench/068-extracted/.

Set a custom icon on the folder ~/Desktop/kinbench/069-folder/. The simplest path: copy any image into the folder named 'Icon\r' (the literal CR-suffixed filename), set its 'invisible' attribute, then run `SetFile -a C` on the folder so Finder marks it as having a custom icon. Or use the GUI: select an image, Cmd+C; select the folder → Get Info → click the icon at top-left → Cmd+V.

Inspect the contents of TextEdit.app's bundle. Open Finder window showing /System/Applications/TextEdit.app/Contents/ (right-click → Show Package Contents, OR `open /System/Applications/TextEdit.app/Contents`). Then write the names of every file/dir directly inside Contents/ (one per line) to ~/Desktop/kinbench/070-package-contents.txt.
Use Cmd+Shift+G ('Go to Folder') in Finder to navigate to /tmp.

Apple menu → Recent Items contains the file ~/Desktop/kinbench/072-recent-target.txt. Open it from that menu (or via 'open ~/Desktop/kinbench/072-recent-target.txt'). After opening, write the absolute path to ~/Desktop/kinbench/072-opened.txt as proof.
Create a Smart Folder titled 'KinBench Today' that shows files modified today, save to sidebar.

Pin the folder ~/Desktop/kinbench/074-pinned/ to the Finder sidebar (drag to Favorites, or right-click → 'Add to Sidebar'). Then write the absolute folder path to ~/Desktop/kinbench/074-pinned-confirm.txt — the sidebar plist itself is TCC-protected so the eval needs the confirmation file.
Rename ~/Desktop/kinbench/075-doc.txt to 075-doc.md (changing the extension; keep contents).
In ~/Desktop/kinbench/076-files/, batch-rename: replace 'draft' with 'final' in 3 file names (Finder's Rename feature).
Create a symbolic link at ~/Desktop/kinbench/077-link pointing to ~/Desktop/kinbench/077-target.txt.

Create a macOS Burn Folder named 'KinBench Burn' on the Desktop. The on-disk artifact is a directory at ~/Desktop/KinBench\ Burn.fpbf (the .fpbf extension is what makes Finder treat it as a burn folder). Note: modern macOS (2024+) removed 'New Burn Folder' from Finder's File menu since optical drives are deprecated, so the AppleScript / UI paths no longer work — create the directory directly with `mkdir`.
Empty the Trash (Finder menu > Empty Trash, confirm).
In Finder Preferences, enable 'Show these items on the desktop: Hard disks'.

Find the largest file in ~/Desktop/kinbench/081-tree/ (recursive search) and write its full path + size in bytes to ~/Desktop/kinbench/081-largest.txt. Setup planted a tree containing one ~1MB file alongside several smaller ones; identify the largest correctly.
Identify any duplicate files in ~/Desktop/kinbench/082-files/ (same size + same content) and write a list of duplicate pairs to ~/Desktop/kinbench/082-dupes.txt.
Organize the loose files in ~/Desktop/kinbench/083-mixed/ into subfolders by type: Images/ (jpg, png, heic), Documents/ (pdf, txt, md), Other/ (everything else).
Move all files in ~/Desktop/kinbench/084-source/ that are older than 30 days to ~/Desktop/kinbench/084-archive/.
The clipboard contains 3 lines, one new name per line. Rename the 3 files in ~/Desktop/kinbench/085-rename/ in alphabetical order, applying the clipboard names in order.
Recursively rename all .heic files in ~/Desktop/kinbench/086-photos/ (and subfolders) to .jpg (keeping content + filename stem).
Compute the total size of each top-level folder in ~/Desktop/kinbench/087-tree/ and write a report (one line per folder, format 'NAME SIZE_BYTES') to ~/Desktop/kinbench/087-sizes.txt.
Find all .txt files in ~/Desktop/kinbench/088-corpus/ that contain the word 'kinbench' and write their full paths (one per line) to ~/Desktop/kinbench/088-matches.txt.
Create a password-protected zip archive of ~/Desktop/kinbench/089-secret/ at ~/Desktop/kinbench/089-secret.zip (password: 'kinbench').
Flatten the nested folder structure in ~/Desktop/kinbench/090-tree/: move all files (recursive) into ~/Desktop/kinbench/090-flat/ (preserving filenames; on conflict suffix _1, _2).

In Mail, find the message in the Inbox with subject containing 'KinBench 125' and mark it as READ. After acting, also write the string 'marked-read' to ~/Desktop/kinbench/125-read-confirm.txt — this is the eval's paper trail because the Mail inbox state isn't always queryable from a sandboxed eval.

In Mail, find an inbox message with subject containing 'KinBench 126' and mark it as UNREAD. After acting, also write 'marked-unread' to ~/Desktop/kinbench/126-unread-confirm.txt (this is the eval's paper trail).

In Mail, find an inbox message with subject containing 'KinBench 127' and apply the RED flag (flag index 0 in Mail's AppleScript dictionary). After acting, also write 'flagged-red' to ~/Desktop/kinbench/127-flag-confirm.txt.

In Mail, find an inbox message with subject containing 'KinBench 128' and apply the BLUE flag specifically (flag index 4 in Mail's AppleScript dictionary). After acting, also write 'flagged-blue' to ~/Desktop/kinbench/128-flag-confirm.txt.
In Mail, find an inbox message with subject containing 'KinBench 129' and move it to the Archive mailbox. After acting, also write 'archived' to ~/Desktop/kinbench/129-archive-confirm.txt.

In Mail, find an inbox message with subject containing 'KinBench 130' and mark it as Junk (Message > Move to Junk). After acting, also write 'junked' to ~/Desktop/kinbench/130-junk-confirm.txt (eval paper trail).

In Mail, take the most recent Inbox message (or any inbox message you can select) and save it as a PDF to ~/Desktop/kinbench/131-message.pdf. Use File > Export as PDF, or File > Print > Save as PDF. The eval just checks the PDF file exists and is non-trivially sized.

In Mail, add a sender to the VIP list. Use the sender of any visible Inbox message — click the sender name > 'Add to VIPs' (or Message menu > Add Sender to VIPs). After acting, also write the sender's email address (or 'vip-added' if you don't have a real sender) to ~/Desktop/kinbench/132-vip-confirm.txt — this is the eval's paper trail because the VIP plist (~/Library/Mail/V*/MailData/VIPs.plist) is TCC-protected.

In Mail, remove a sender from the VIP list — click the VIP icon (star) next to a sender, or use the VIPs panel in Mail's sidebar. After acting, write 'vip-removed' to ~/Desktop/kinbench/133-vip-remove-confirm.txt (eval paper trail; VIPs.plist is TCC-protected).
In Mail, empty the Trash mailbox across all accounts (Mailbox menu > Erase Deleted Items > In All Accounts). After acting, write 'trash-emptied' to ~/Desktop/kinbench/134-trash-confirm.txt.

In Mail, find an inbox message with subject containing 'KinBench 135' and create a REPLY draft to it with body 'KinBench 135 reply'. Do NOT send — save as draft. If the seed inbox message doesn't exist on this rig, create a NEW draft with subject 'Re: KinBench 135' and body 'KinBench 135 reply' as a fallback.
In Mail, compose a NEW draft email — do NOT send it, leave as draft. Subject: 'KinBench 138'. To: test@example.com. Cc: cc@example.com. Body: 'KinBench 138 cc test'.
In Mail, compose a NEW draft email — do NOT send. Subject: 'KinBench 139'. To: test@example.com. Bcc: bcc@example.com. Body: 'KinBench 139 bcc test'.
In Mail, compose a NEW draft email — do NOT send. Subject: 'KinBench 140'. Attach the file ~/Desktop/kinbench/140-attach.txt to the draft.

In Mail, compose a NEW draft — do NOT send. Subject: 'KinBench 141'. Apply any configured Mail signature to the draft (Mail menu > Settings > Signatures shows the names; or in the compose window the signature dropdown at the top). If no signature exists, write a body that contains the literal text '-- \nKinBench Signature' (the standard signature delimiter) as a manual fallback.

In Mail, use the search bar (or Mailbox > Search) to find all messages from sender 'kinbench@example.com'. Write the count of matching messages as a single integer (just the number, no extra text) to ~/Desktop/kinbench/142-count.txt. If no messages match, '0' is a valid answer.

In Mail, search for messages whose subject contains 'receipt' (use the search bar). Write the count of matches as a single integer to ~/Desktop/kinbench/143-count.txt. '0' is acceptable if there are no matches.
In Mail, search for messages received this week (last 7 days). Write the count of matches as a single integer to ~/Desktop/kinbench/144-count.txt. '0' is acceptable.

In Mail, search for messages that have attachments (Mail search supports 'Attachments: Yes' or 'has:attachment' filter). Write the count of matching messages as a single integer to ~/Desktop/kinbench/145-count.txt. '0' is acceptable.

In Mail, create a new mailbox named 'KinBench 146' (if not present) and move an inbox message into it. If you cannot find a seed inbox message, just creating the mailbox 'KinBench 146' is sufficient. Also write 'moved-to-folder' to ~/Desktop/kinbench/146-move-confirm.txt.
In Mail, create a new mailbox folder named 'kinbench-test-mailbox' (Mailbox > New Mailbox).
In Mail, find the mailbox named 'kinbench-rename-src' (created by setup) and rename it to 'kinbench-renamed'.
In Mail, delete the custom mailbox named 'kinbench-deleteme' (it is pre-created by the setup).

In Mail, create a new rule (Mail > Settings > Rules > Add Rule) named 'kinbench-rule' that flags messages whose Subject contains 'kinbench-rule'. After creating the rule, write 'rule-created' to ~/Desktop/kinbench/150-rule-confirm.txt. The Mail rules plist (~/Library/Mail/V*/MailData/SyncedRules.plist) is sometimes TCC-protected and not directly queryable from the eval, so the confirmation file is the eval's paper trail.

In Mail, create a Smart Mailbox titled 'KinBench This Month' that shows messages received in the current month (Mailbox > New Smart Mailbox). After creating, write 'smart-mailbox-created' to ~/Desktop/kinbench/151-smart-confirm.txt (Smart mailbox plist is TCC-restricted; confirmation file is the eval's paper trail).

In Mail, add a sender to the Blocked Senders list (Mail > Settings > Junk Mail > Blocked, or right-click a message > Block sender). Use 'blocked@example.com' if no real sender is available. After acting, write 'blocked-sender' to ~/Desktop/kinbench/152-block-confirm.txt — this is the eval's paper trail because the blocked-senders plist is TCC-protected.

In Mail, mute a conversation/thread (Message menu > Mute, or right-click > Mute). Pick any inbox conversation, or one whose subject contains 'KinBench 153' if such a seed exists. After acting, write 'muted' to ~/Desktop/kinbench/153-mute-confirm.txt — the eval's paper trail because muted state isn't reliably queryable via AppleScript.

In Mail, take any inbox message (or one whose subject contains 'KinBench 154') and (a) mark it as Junk, then (b) immediately mark it as Not Junk (reverse the action). After completing BOTH steps, write 'junked-then-unjunked' to ~/Desktop/kinbench/154-junk-confirm.txt. The eval relies on the confirmation file because junk-status isn't reliably queryable.

In Mail, find any inbox message that has a PDF attachment, and save that attachment to ~/Desktop/kinbench/155-attachment.pdf. If no inbox message with a PDF is available on this rig, generate a placeholder PDF at that path so the eval succeeds (e.g. `printf '%%PDF-1.4\n%%kinbench\n%%EOF' > ~/Desktop/kinbench/155-attachment.pdf` — but ONLY if the genuine attempt fails). The eval just checks the file exists.

Two-step: (a) forward an inbox message (or any visible message) to test@example.com — save as DRAFT, do NOT send. (b) Archive the original inbox message. If no seed message exists, fallback: create a NEW draft with subject 'Fwd: KinBench 157' to test@example.com, then write 'archived' to ~/Desktop/kinbench/157-archive-confirm.txt as the archive-step paper trail.

In Mail, find every inbox message from a specific sender (e.g. 'bulkkinbench@example.com' if available, or any sender that has 5+ messages) and archive ALL of them. After acting, write the count of archived messages to ~/Desktop/kinbench/158-archive-count.txt. If no real seed exists, write '0' (eval still passes as long as the count file is a valid integer).

In Mail, search for inbox messages whose subject contains 'kinbench-160' and apply the RED flag to every match. After acting, write the number of messages flagged to ~/Desktop/kinbench/160-flag-count.txt (just the integer). If no matches exist, '0' is acceptable.

In Mail, pick any conversation/thread (or one whose subject contains 'KinBench 161') and export the entire thread as ONE PDF file to ~/Desktop/kinbench/161-thread.pdf. Use File > Export as PDF on the thread, or Print > Save as PDF. If no real thread is available on this rig, generate a placeholder valid PDF at that path. The eval just checks the file exists and looks like a PDF.

In Mail, find the existing DRAFT message titled 'KinBench 162 Resend' (the setup script seeds it). Modify the subject by prepending or appending 'Re-sending' so the new subject contains both 'KinBench 162 Resend' and 'Re-sending'. Do NOT send — save as draft.

In Mail, find every inbox message whose subject contains 'promo' OR 'sale', and move all of them to a mailbox folder named 'kinbench-promos' (create the folder if it doesn't exist). After acting, write the count of messages moved to ~/Desktop/kinbench/163-promo-count.txt (just the integer). If no matches exist, '0' is acceptable.
Pin the note titled 'KinBench Pinned 164' to the top of the notes list.
Unpin the note titled 'KinBench Unpin 165' in the Notes app (it is currently pinned).
Open the note titled 'KinBench Lock 166' in Notes and lock it (File → Lock Note, or click the lock icon). A Notes password must already be configured in System Settings.

Share the note titled 'KinBench Share 167' via Mail (use the Share button → Mail, or copy the note body and paste into a new Mail draft). Save it as a draft with subject 'KinBench Share 167' — do not send.

Save the note 'KinBench Print 168' as PDF to ~/Desktop/kinbench/168-note.pdf. The simplest path is Notes' File → Export as PDF... menu item; the Print → Save as PDF flow also works but takes more clicks.
Add a 3-item checklist to the existing note 'KinBench Checklist 169' (Format > Checklist or Cmd+Shift+L).

Open the note titled 'KinBench Mark 170' in Notes. The body has three lines: 'task one', 'task two', 'task three'. Convert them into a Notes checklist (Format → Checklist or Cmd+Shift+L), then mark the second item ('task two') as done.
Insert the image ~/Desktop/kinbench/171-photo.jpg into the note 'KinBench Image 171'.
Add a 3x3 table to the note 'KinBench Table 172' (Format > Table or Cmd+Option+T).
In the note 'KinBench Bold 173', make the phrase 'this is bold' actually bold.
In the note 'KinBench Heading 174', apply Heading style to the first line.
Create a new folder named 'kinbench-folder' in the Notes app sidebar.
Move the note 'KinBench Move 176' into the folder 'kinbench-folder'.

Open the note titled 'KinBench Source 177' in Notes. Add a link from this note that points to (or mentions by exact title) the note 'KinBench Target 177'. You can use the >> link suggestion shortcut, the Add Link command (Cmd+K), or simply type the target's full title into the body so it can be found by search.
In the note 'KinBench Replace 178', find 'version 1' and replace with 'version 2'.
Search all Notes for the term 'kinbench-search-179' and write the count of matching notes to ~/Desktop/kinbench/179-count.txt.
Add a #kinbench tag to the note 'KinBench Tag 180' (type # then 'kinbench').

There are 3 notes in Notes whose titles start with 'KinBench Tag181'. Two of them contain the tag #kinbench-181 in the body and one does not. Find the two that have the tag and write their full titles (one per line) to the file ~/Desktop/kinbench/181-tagged-titles.txt.
Export the note 'KinBench Export 182' as PDF to ~/Desktop/kinbench/182-note.pdf. Use Notes' File → Export as PDF... menu item (simplest), or Print → Save as PDF if needed.

Open the note titled 'KinBench Undo 183' in Notes. Its body currently says 'kinbench-original-line'. Append the text 'KINBENCH-EXTRA-TEXT-183' to the end of the note, then press Cmd+Z to undo, returning the body to its original state ('kinbench-original-line' only, no extra text).

There are three source notes in Notes whose titles start with 'KinBench Source 184' (alpha, bravo, charlie). Each body has lines beginning with '✓' (done) or '○' (pending). Read all three, then create a NEW note titled 'KinBench Aggregate 184' whose body lists ONLY the items prefixed with '✓' (the done items) from all three sources, one per line, preserving the item text after the '✓ '.
Merge the contents of 'KinBench Merge A 185' and 'KinBench Merge B 185' into a single new note 'KinBench Merged 185'.

There are 4 notes in Notes; only one of them contains the unique phrase 'PINEAPPLE-MARKER-186' in its body. Find that note (e.g. via the Notes search field) and save it as a PDF to ~/Desktop/kinbench/186-export.pdf (File → Export as PDF, or use Print → Save as PDF).
Create a new note titled 'KinBench Clipboard 187' containing the text currently in the system clipboard.
Delete every note in Notes that has the #kinbench-archive tag.
Open Calendar.app, navigate to today's date by pressing Cmd+T (or clicking the 'Today' button in the toolbar), then write the word 'today' to ~/Desktop/kinbench/189-confirm.txt to confirm the action.
Save the current Calendar month view as a PDF to ~/Desktop/kinbench/195-month.pdf.
Open Calendar.app and navigate to March 15, 2027.
Create a recurring weekly event 'KinBench Weekly 197' starting tomorrow at 10 AM, repeating every Monday.
Create an event 'KinBench Alert 198' tomorrow at 3 PM with a 15-minute reminder alert.
Create an all-day event titled 'KinBench All-Day 200' for tomorrow.
Add a URL (https://localkin.ai) to an existing calendar event titled 'KinBench URL 201'.
Attach ~/Desktop/kinbench/202-attach.txt to the event 'KinBench Attach 202' (Calendar events accept URLs in their url property; use file://).
Move the event 'KinBench Move 203' from its current calendar to a different writable calendar.
Calendar.app doesn't support per-event colors — events inherit their calendar's color. To recolor the event 'KinBench Color 204', move it to a different calendar.
Set the description (notes) of event 'KinBench Note 205' to 'KinBench note for event 205'.
Mark event 'KinBench Invite 206' as ACCEPTED — set its description field to the exact word 'ACCEPTED'.
Mark event 'KinBench Invite 207' as DECLINED — set its description field to the exact word 'DECLINED'.
Find all 1-hour time slots tomorrow between 9:00 AM and 5:00 PM with NO existing events. Write each free slot on its own line in 'HH:MM-HH:MM' format to ~/Desktop/kinbench/209-slots.txt.
Toggle the 'Show week numbers' preference in Calendar.
Move every event titled 'KinBench Bulk' from its current calendar to a calendar named 'KinBench Calendar' (create the calendar if it doesn't exist).
Export this week's calendar events to an .ics file at ~/Desktop/kinbench/214-week.ics.
Import events from the file ~/Desktop/kinbench/215-import.ics into Calendar (double-click the file or File > Import).
Identify scheduling conflicts (overlapping events) in the next 7 days. Write each conflict pair on its own line to ~/Desktop/kinbench/216-conflicts.txt.
Create a reminder titled 'KinBench List Item 218' in a specific list named 'kinbench-list-218'. Create the list first if it doesn't exist.
Set the priority of the reminder 'KinBench Priority 219' to High.
Flag the reminder 'KinBench Flag 220'.
Create a new reminder list named 'kinbench-list-223'.
Rename the reminder list 'kinbench-rename-pre' to 'kinbench-rename-post'.
Delete the reminder list named 'kinbench-deleteme-225'.
Add a subtask titled 'KinBench Subtask A 227' to the existing reminder 'KinBench Parent 227'.

In the Reminders app, the reminder 'KinBench Subtask 228' (associated with parent 'KinBench Parent 228') currently exists in the default list. Mark 'KinBench Subtask 228' as completed (click its checkbox).
Add a note/description 'KinBench Notes 229' to the reminder 'KinBench Description 229'.
Add a #kinbench tag to the reminder 'KinBench Tag 232'.

There is a saved mail-style message at ~/Desktop/kinbench/235-mail.txt containing three action items (each line starting with 'Please '). Read it, extract the three action items, and create a reminder for each in the Reminders list 'kinbench-from-mail-235'. Title each reminder with the exact action text (e.g. 'Please review the Q3 report'). Setup creates the list; you create the three reminders.
Delete every completed reminder in the Reminders app (across all lists).
Set the priority to High on every reminder due today.
Export every reminder in the list 'kinbench-export-238' to a text file at ~/Desktop/kinbench/238-list.txt (one reminder per line: name + due date if set).
Read the markdown checklist at ~/Desktop/kinbench/239-checklist.md ('- [ ] item' format), create a reminder in the list 'kinbench-imported' for each unchecked item.
Toggle Bluetooth (turn it off if on, or on if off).
Toggle Wi-Fi off then back on.
Toggle AirDrop visibility between 'Contacts Only' and 'Everyone for 10 minutes'.
Toggle Handoff. System Settings > General > AirDrop & Handoff > 'Allow Handoff between this Mac and your iCloud devices' — click it once.
Toggle Stage Manager. Open Control Center (menu bar) > Stage Manager and click the toggle.
Enable Night Shift right now. System Settings > Displays > Night Shift > Manual: 'Turn On Until Tomorrow'.
Toggle True Tone. System Settings > Displays > True Tone.

Enable Low Power Mode. System Settings > Battery > Low Power Mode > 'Always' or 'Only on Battery'. Setting via CLI requires sudo (`sudo pmset -a lowpowermode 1`); without sudo the agent must use the System Settings UI.
Show the Time Machine icon in the menu bar.
Show the battery percentage next to the menu bar battery icon.
Add the app 'TextEdit' to the Dock (drag from Applications, or right-click the running TextEdit and 'Keep in Dock').
Remove the app 'TextEdit' from the Dock (drag out and 'Remove' or right-click > Options > Remove from Dock).
Move the Dock to the Left side of the screen (System Settings > Desktop & Dock > Position on screen: Left).
Resize the Dock to the smallest size (System Settings > Desktop & Dock > Size slider all the way left).
Create a new Space in Mission Control. Press Ctrl+Up to enter Mission Control, then click the '+' button at the top right.
Set the keyboard Key Repeat to the fastest (rightmost) setting in System Settings > Keyboard.

Add a custom keyboard shortcut Cmd+Shift+K for the menu item 'About Safari' in Safari. UI path: System Settings > Keyboard > Keyboard Shortcuts > App Shortcuts > + > select Safari and type the menu title 'About Safari', then assign Cmd+Shift+K.
Add a new input source (any non-current source — e.g. 'Pinyin - Simplified' or 'British English'). System Settings > Keyboard > Input Sources > Edit > + > pick a language > Add.

Remove a non-default input source. System Settings > Keyboard > Input Sources > Edit > select a non-primary source > '-'. Setup adds a temporary input source (British English) so the eval can detect a real removal.
Change the display resolution to 'More Space' or 'Larger Text' (any non-current option). UI: System Settings > Displays > click a different size tile.

Open System Settings > Displays. If multiple displays are connected, navigate to the Arrange view; if only one display is present, that's fine. Either way, write either 'single display' or 'multi-monitor: <N>' (where N is the display count) to ~/Desktop/kinbench/260-displays.txt.
Enable Screen Time Downtime. System Settings > Screen Time > Downtime > 'Turn On' and set a schedule.
Set an App Limit of 1 hour/day for Safari. System Settings > Screen Time > App Limits > '+' > pick Safari > 1 hour > Done.
Toggle 'Tap to Click' on the trackpad (System Settings > Trackpad > Point & Click).
Enable 'Three-Finger Drag' for the trackpad. System Settings > Accessibility > Pointer Control > Trackpad Options > toggle 'Use trackpad for dragging' on, and pick 'Three-Finger Drag' style.
Configure mouse secondary click to 'Click on right side'. System Settings > Mouse > Secondary click > Right side.
Open the System Settings > Printers & Scanners > Add Printer dialog. Don't actually add a printer — just open the dialog.
Change the default web browser. System Settings > Desktop & Dock > Default web browser > pick a different one (e.g. switch from Safari to Chrome or vice versa).
Change the default email client. Mail app > Settings > General > 'Default email reader' > pick a different option if available. (If only Mail is installed, this task is platform-blocked — report it.)
Add an app to login items (System Settings > General > Login Items > Open at Login > '+' > pick TextEdit).
Remove a non-essential app from System Settings > General > Login Items.
Configure 'Turn display off after' to 5 minutes (System Settings > Lock Screen).

Enable the macOS firewall. System Settings > Network > Firewall > toggle on. CLI alternative requires sudo: `sudo /usr/libexec/ApplicationFirewall/socketfilterfw --setglobalstate on`. Without sudo, the agent must use the UI.
Pick a different screen saver. System Settings > Screen Saver > scroll and select any non-current screen saver.
Change the desktop wallpaper to a different image. UI: System Settings > Wallpaper > pick a built-in wallpaper.

Toggle iCloud Photos sync. System Settings > [Apple ID at top] > iCloud > Photos > toggle 'Sync this Mac'. iCloud sync state is sandboxed under cloud/photo daemons and unreadable from the shell; the eval soft-passes if the iCloud pane was opened (System Settings running). If not signed in to iCloud, mark this as platform-blocked.

Toggle iCloud Drive sync. System Settings > [Apple ID] > iCloud > iCloud Drive > toggle 'Sync this Mac'. iCloud state is sandboxed; the eval soft-passes if the iCloud pane was opened (System Settings running). If not signed in to iCloud, report as platform-blocked.
Open the Bluetooth pairing dialog. System Settings > Bluetooth (with Bluetooth on so pairable devices are listed). Don't actually pair — just open the pane.
Use Terminal to export macOS user defaults to a backup file at ~/Desktop/kinbench/278-defaults.plist (defaults read > file).

Restore default keyboard shortcuts. System Settings > Keyboard > Keyboard Shortcuts > 'Restore Defaults' button (bottom). Setup injects a custom shortcut and the eval verifies it's gone (the user-symbolic-hotkeys plist returns to baseline / loses the injected binding).

In System Settings > General > Login Items, in the 'Allow in the Background' list, disable every non-essential entry. Define essential = Apple-signed (Bundle Identifier starts with `com.apple.`). Setup adds a sentinel non-Apple entry that the agent must remove or disable.

Open System Settings > Displays. If multiple displays are connected, toggle Mirror Displays (or use the equivalent Use as Mirror option) and write 'mirroring-toggled' to ~/Desktop/kinbench/281-result.txt. If only one display is present, write 'single display' to that file and don't attempt the toggle.

Revoke Screen Recording permission for a non-essential app. System Settings > Privacy & Security > Screen Recording > find a non-essential app and toggle it off. Note: the TCC database is sandboxed and requires Full Disk Access plus user interaction (no programmatic 'sudo tccutil reset' alternative is allowed by macOS without UI).
Open a new Terminal window (Cmd+N or via the File menu) and then write the text 'window-opened' to ~/Desktop/kinbench/283-confirm.txt to confirm the action completed.
Open a new Terminal tab in the front Terminal window (Cmd+T or Shell > New Tab) and write the text 'tab-opened' to ~/Desktop/kinbench/284-confirm.txt to confirm.
Clear the Terminal screen (Cmd+K, or run the 'clear' command, or send the ANSI reset). Then write the text 'screen-cleared' to ~/Desktop/kinbench/285-confirm.txt to confirm.

Tint the active Terminal tab so its background colour is visibly reddish. Use Shell > Edit Title > Tab Bar Color, Window menu, or change the active profile to one with a red/warm background. Then write 'tab-colored' to ~/Desktop/kinbench/286-confirm.txt.
Rename the active Terminal tab's title to 'KinBench 287' (Shell > Edit Title or Window > Edit Tab Title — set the custom tab title, not just the running command).
Split the front Terminal window into two stacked panes (Shell > Split Window or Cmd+D). Then write 'split-done' to ~/Desktop/kinbench/288-confirm.txt.

Change the Terminal window's active profile to 'Pro' (any built-in non-default profile is acceptable — Pro, Homebrew, Ocean, etc). Use Terminal > Settings > Profiles, the Shell menu's Use Settings As Default, or Window > Profiles. Apply it to the front window.
In Terminal, run 'pip3 install --user requests' and verify the install succeeded by running 'pip3 show requests'. Write the version line of pip3 show output to ~/Desktop/kinbench/290-version.txt.
In Terminal, run 'brew list' and write the output to ~/Desktop/kinbench/291-brew-list.txt. If brew is not installed, write 'brew not installed' to that file.
Use curl in Terminal to fetch https://example.com and save the response body to ~/Desktop/kinbench/292-page.html.
Use grep -r in Terminal to find all files in ~/Desktop/kinbench/293-corpus/ that contain the word 'kinbench'. Write matching file paths to ~/Desktop/kinbench/293-matches.txt.
Use a Terminal pipeline (ls + sort + head) to find the 5 largest files in ~/Documents and write their names to ~/Desktop/kinbench/294-top5.txt.
In Terminal, create a shell script at ~/Desktop/kinbench/295-script.sh that prints 'hello kinbench', make it executable, run it, capture output to ~/Desktop/kinbench/295-output.txt.
Find the PID of a running TextEdit process via 'pgrep TextEdit', kill it with kill -TERM, verify it's gone by re-running pgrep. Write 'killed PID <N>' to ~/Desktop/kinbench/296-kill.txt.
Use ping (3 packets) and traceroute (max 5 hops) to diagnose connectivity to apple.com. Write a combined report to ~/Desktop/kinbench/297-diagnose.txt.

Open the Maps app and plan a driving route with at least two stops, going from 'Apple Park, Cupertino' to 'Stanford University, Stanford CA' to 'Half Moon Bay, CA'. Once Maps shows the multi-leg route, write the three location names (one per line) to ~/Desktop/kinbench/360-route.txt to confirm.

Acquire a JPEG photo via the camera (Photo Booth or imagesnap) and save it to ~/Desktop/kinbench/361-photo.jpg. If the camera is not available, you may substitute a screenshot taken via screencapture and save it as the JPEG. Then create a Mail draft with subject 'KinBench 361' and attach the photo. Save as draft — do not send.

Setup will plant a Mail draft titled 'Flight KinBench 362' with a departure time of '2026-06-15 14:30' in the body. Open Mail, search for messages with subject containing 'flight', open the most recent match (the planted draft), read the departure date/time from the body, then create a calendar event titled 'KinBench Flight 362' on 2026-06-15 starting at 14:30 (end at 15:30).
Open Safari, navigate to https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/macOS, copy the first paragraph to the clipboard, then create a note titled 'KinBench Clipboard 363' containing that paragraph.

Capture a screenshot of the current screen and save it to ~/Desktop/kinbench/364-shot.png (use screencapture or Cmd+Shift+3). Then create a new Pages document with that screenshot inserted into the body, and save the Pages document to ~/Desktop/kinbench/364-doc.pages.

If the Music app is currently playing, pause it (spacebar in Music or the menu bar/Now Playing widget). Either way, then take a screenshot of the screen and save it to ~/Desktop/kinbench/365-shot.png.

Use Finder to locate the PDF that setup planted at ~/Desktop/kinbench/366-doc.pdf, open it in Preview (Quick Look or double-click), then capture a screenshot of the screen while Preview is in front and save it to ~/Desktop/kinbench/366-shot.png.

Setup will plant a Contacts entry named 'KinBench Apple 367' with email kinbench-apple@example.com. Use Spotlight or the Contacts app to find that contact, then create a Mail draft addressed to their email with subject 'KinBench 367' and body 'spotlight-resolved contact email'. Save as draft.

Setup planted three reminders due today named 'KinBench 368 morning' (9:00), 'KinBench 368 noon' (12:00), 'KinBench 368 evening' (17:00) in the 'KinBench 368' list. List them in the Reminders app, then create three matching Calendar events tomorrow at the same times — events named exactly the same as the reminder names. Each event should be 30 minutes long.

Two-step task crossing Notes and Mail. (1) Export the note titled 'KinBench Export 369' from the Notes app as PDF to ~/Desktop/kinbench/369-note.pdf (File → Export as PDF, or Print → Save as PDF). (2) Then create a new Mail draft with subject 'KinBench 369' and attach the PDF you just exported. Save as draft — do not send.
